subcommands.name: container run
subcommands.description: Create and run a new container from an image

arguments.name: Annotation
arguments.description: Add an annotation to the container (passed through to the OCI runtime)

arguments.name: Attach
arguments.description: Attach to STDIN, STDOUT or STDERR

arguments.name: Block IO Weight
arguments.description: Block IO weight (relative weight), between 10 and 1000, or 0 to disable

arguments.name: Cgroup Parent
arguments.description: Optional parent cgroup for the container

arguments.name: CID File
arguments.description: Write the container ID to the file

arguments.name: CPU Period
arguments.description: Limit CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) period

arguments.name: CPU Quota
arguments.description: Limit CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) quota

arguments.name: CPU RT Period
arguments.description: Limit CPU real-time period in microseconds

arguments.name: CPU RT Runtime
arguments.description: Limit CPU real-time runtime in microseconds

arguments.name: CPUset CPUs
arguments.description: CPUs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)

arguments.name: CPUset Mems
arguments.description: MEMs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)

arguments.name: Detach
arguments.description: Run container in background and print container ID

arguments.name: Detach Keys
arguments.description: Override the key sequence for detaching a container

arguments.name: Device
arguments.description: Add a host device to the container

arguments.name: Device Cgroup Rule
arguments.description: Add a rule to the cgroup allowed devices list

arguments.name: Device Read BPS
arguments.description: Limit read rate (bytes per second) from a device

arguments.name: Device Read IOPS
arguments.description: Limit read rate (IO per second) from a device

arguments.name: Device Write BPS
arguments.description: Limit write rate (bytes per second) to a device

arguments.name: Device Write IOPS
arguments.description: Limit write rate (IO per second) to a device

arguments.name: Entrypoint
arguments.description: Overwrite the default ENTRYPOINT of the image

arguments.name: Env File
arguments.description: Read in a file of environment variables

arguments.name: Expose
arguments.description: Expose a port or a range of ports

arguments.name: GPUs
arguments.description: GPU devices to add to the container ('all' to pass all GPUs)

arguments.name: Health Cmd
arguments.description: Command to run to check health

arguments.name: Health Retries
arguments.description: Consecutive failures needed to report unhealthy

arguments.name: Health Start Interval
arguments.description: Time between running the check during the start period (ms|s|m|h)

arguments.name: Health Start Period
arguments.description: Start period for the container to initialize before starting health-retries countdown (ms|s|m|h)

arguments.name: Health Timeout
arguments.description: Maximum time to allow one check to run (ms|s|m|h)

arguments.name: Init
arguments.description: Run an init inside the container that forwards signals and reaps processes

arguments.name: Interactive
arguments.description: Keep STDIN open even if not attached

arguments.name: Label File
arguments.description: Read in a line delimited file of labels

arguments.name: Memory Swap
arguments.description: Swap limit equal to memory plus swap: '-1' to enable unlimited swap

arguments.name: Mount
arguments.description: Attach a filesystem mount to the container

arguments.name: Name
arguments.description: Assign a name to the container

arguments.name: Network
arguments.description: Connect a container to a network

arguments.name: Network Alias
arguments.description: Add network-scoped alias for the container

arguments.name: PIDs Limit
arguments.description: Tune container pids limit (set -1 for unlimited)

arguments.name: Platform
arguments.description: Set platform if server is multi-platform capable

arguments.name: Privileged
arguments.description: Give extended privileges to this container

arguments.name: Publish
arguments.description: Publish a container's port(s) to the host

arguments.name: Publish All
arguments.description: Publish all exposed ports to random ports

arguments.name: Read Only
arguments.description: Mount the container's root filesystem as read only

arguments.name: Restart
arguments.description: Restart policy to apply when a container exits

arguments.name: Remove
arguments.description: Automatically remove the container when it exits

arguments.name: Runtime
arguments.description: Runtime to use for this container

arguments.name: Sig Proxy
arguments.description: Proxy received signals to the process

arguments.name: Stop Timeout
arguments.description: Timeout (in seconds) to stop a container

arguments.name: Storage Opt
arguments.description: Storage driver options for the container

arguments.name: Volume Driver
arguments.description: Optional volume driver for the container

arguments.name: Volumes From
arguments.description: Mount volumes from the specified container(s)

subcommands.name: container exec
subcommands.description: Execute a command in a running container

arguments.name: Detach
arguments.description: Detached mode: run command in the background

arguments.name: Detach Keys
arguments.description: Override the key sequence for detaching a container

arguments.name: Env File
arguments.description: Read in a file of environment variables

arguments.name: Interactive
arguments.description: Keep STDIN open even if not attached

arguments.name: Privileged
arguments.description: Give extended privileges to the command

subcommands.name: container ls
subcommands.description: List containers

arguments.name: All
arguments.description: Show all containers (default shows just running)

arguments.name: Filter
arguments.description: Filter output based on conditions provided

arguments.name: Format
arguments.description: Format output using a custom template

arguments.name: Last
arguments.description: Show n last created containers (includes all states)

arguments.name: Latest
arguments.description: Show the latest created container (includes all states)

subcommands.name: container logs
subcommands.description: Fetch the logs of a container

arguments.name: Details
arguments.description: Show extra details provided to logs

arguments.name: Since
arguments.description: Show logs since timestamp or relative time

arguments.name: Tail
arguments.description: Number of lines to show from the end of the logs

arguments.name: Until
arguments.description: Show logs before a timestamp or relative time

subcommands.name: container stop
subcommands.description: Stop one or more running containers

arguments.name: Signal
arguments.description: Signal to send to the container

arguments.name: Time
arguments.description: Seconds to wait before killing the container

subcommands.name: container start
subcommands.description: Start one or more stopped containers

arguments.name: Detach Keys
arguments.description: Override the key sequence for detaching a container

subcommands.name: container restart
subcommands.description: Restart one or more containers

arguments.name: Signal
arguments.description: Signal to send to the container

arguments.name: Time
arguments.description: Seconds to wait before killing the container

subcommands.name: container kill
subcommands.description: Kill one or more running containers

arguments.name: Signal
arguments.description: Signal to send to the container

subcommands.name: container rm
subcommands.description: Remove one or more containers

arguments.name: Force
arguments.description: Force the removal of a running container (uses SIGKILL)

arguments.name: Volumes
arguments.description: Remove anonymous volumes associated with the container

subcommands.name: container pause
subcommands.description: Pause all processes within one or more containers

subcommands.name: container unpause
subcommands.description: Unpause all processes within one or more containers

subcommands.name: container inspect
subcommands.description: Display detailed information on one or more containers

arguments.name: Format
arguments.description: Format output using a custom template

subcommands.name: container cp
subcommands.description: Copy files/folders between a container and the local filesystem

arguments.name: Follow Link
arguments.description: Always follow symbol link in SRC_PATH

subcommands.name: container commit
subcommands.description: Create a new image from a container's changes

arguments.name: Change
arguments.description: Apply Dockerfile instruction to the created image

subcommands.name: container diff
subcommands.description: Inspect changes to files or directories on a container's filesystem

subcommands.name: container export
subcommands.description: Export a container's filesystem as a tar archive

arguments.name: Output
arguments.description: Write to a file, instead of STDOUT

subcommands.name: container port
subcommands.description: List port mappings or a specific mapping for the container

subcommands.name: container prune
subcommands.description: Remove all stopped containers

subcommands.name: container rename
subcommands.description: Rename a container

subcommands.name: container stats
subcommands.description: Display a live stream of container resource usage statistics

arguments.name: All
arguments.description: Show all containers (default shows just running)

arguments.name: Format
arguments.description: Format output using a custom template

arguments.name: No Stream
arguments.description: Disable streaming stats and only pull the first result

subcommands.name: container top
subcommands.description: Display the running processes of a container

subcommands.name: container update
subcommands.description: Update configuration of one or more containers

arguments.name: Block IO Weight
arguments.description: Block IO weight (relative weight), between 10 and 1000, or 0 to disable

arguments.name: CPU Period
arguments.description: Limit CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) period

arguments.name: CPU Quota
arguments.description: Limit CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) quota

arguments.name: CPU RT Period
arguments.description: Limit the CPU real-time period in microseconds

arguments.name: CPU RT Runtime
arguments.description: Limit the CPU real-time runtime in microseconds

arguments.name: CPUset CPUs
arguments.description: CPUs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)

arguments.name: CPUset Mems
arguments.description: MEMs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)

arguments.name: Memory Swap
arguments.description: Swap limit equal to memory plus swap (-1 for unlimited)

arguments.name: PIDs Limit
arguments.description: Tune container pids limit (-1 for unlimited)

arguments.name: Restart
arguments.description: Restart policy to apply when a container exits

subcommands.name: container wait
subcommands.description: Block until one or more containers stop, then print their exit codes

subcommands.name: container attach
subcommands.description: Attach local standard input, output, and error streams to a running container

arguments.name: Detach Keys
arguments.description: Override the key sequence for detaching a container

arguments.name: Sig Proxy
arguments.description: Proxy all received signals to the process

subcommands.name: image build
subcommands.description: Build an image from a Dockerfile

arguments.name: Cache From
arguments.description: Images to consider as cache sources

arguments.name: Cgroup Parent
arguments.description: Set the parent cgroup for RUN instructions

arguments.name: Compress
arguments.description: Compress the build context using gzip

arguments.name: CPU Period
arguments.description: Limit CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) period

arguments.name: CPU Quota
arguments.description: Limit CPU CFS (Completely Fair Scheduler) quota

arguments.name: CPUset CPUs
arguments.description: CPUs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)

arguments.name: CPUset Mems
arguments.description: MEMs in which to allow execution (0-3, 0,1)

arguments.name: Iidfile
arguments.description: Write the image ID to the file

arguments.name: Memory Swap
arguments.description: Swap limit equal to memory plus swap (-1 for unlimited)

arguments.name: Network
arguments.description: Set the networking mode for RUN instructions

arguments.name: No Cache
arguments.description: Do not use cache when building the image

arguments.name: Platform
arguments.description: Set platform if server is multi-platform capable

arguments.name: Pull
arguments.description: Always attempt to pull a newer version of the image

arguments.name: Quiet
arguments.description: Suppress the build output and print image ID on success

arguments.name: Remove
arguments.description: Remove intermediate containers after a successful build

arguments.name: Squash
arguments.description: Squash newly built layers into a single new layer

arguments.name: Target
arguments.description: Set the target build stage to build
